meta.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Design, a new product for generating designs, prototypes, slides, and one-pagers with Claude, extending the company from chat and coding into visual creation workflows. ByteDance Seed also surfaced multiple concrete model artifacts — Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM — while Baidu’s open-weight ERNIE-Image claimed state-of-the-art text-to-image performance with an 8B-parameter DiT, signaling continued competition in multimodal foundation models.

items.title: Introducing granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock
items.summary_short: In this post, we share how Amazon Bedrock's granular cost attribution works and walk through example cost tracking scenarios

items.title: Optimize video semantic search intent with Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Amazon Bedrock
items.summary_short: In this post, we show you how to use Model Distillation, a model customization technique on Amazon Bedrock, to transfer routing intelligence from a large teacher model (Amazon Nova Premier) into a much smaller student model (Amazon Nova Micro).

items.title: Power video semantic search with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
items.summary_short: In this post, we show you how to build a video semantic search solution on Amazon Bedrock using Nova Multimodal Embeddings that intelligently understands user intent and retrieves accurate video results across all signal types simultaneously.

items.title: Nova Forge SDK series part 2: Practical guide to fine-tune Nova models using data mixing capabilities
items.summary_short: This hands-on guide walks through every step of fine-tuning an Amazon Nova model with the Amazon Nova Forge SDK, from data preparation to training with data mixing to evaluation, giving you a repeatable playbook you can adapt to your own use case.

source_sections.label: Research Papers
source_sections.description: Research papers and paper-trending signals tied to the current day.

items.title: A Mechanistic Account of Attention Sinks in GPT-2: One Circuit, Broader Implications for Mitigation
items.summary_short: Transformers commonly exhibit an attention sink: disproportionately high attention to the first position. We study this behavior in GPT-2-style models with learned query biases and absolute positional embeddings.

topic_refs.headline: A Mechanistic Account of Attention Sinks in GPT-2: One Circuit, Broader Implications for Mitigation

items.title: On the Expressive Power and Limitations of Multi-Layer SSMs
items.summary_short: A research paper analyzes the expressive power and limitations of multi-layer state space models. The summary also notes online chain-of-thought as a mechanism discussed alongside these expressivity results

topic_refs.headline: On the Expressive Power and Limitations of Multi-Layer SSMs

items.spotlight_comment: The combination of rigorous SSM expressivity results with online CoT as a capability-transforming mechanism feels like a potentially new foundational direction.

items.title: Gating Enables Curvature: A Geometric Expressivity Gap in Attention
items.summary_short: A research paper develops a geometric theory for attention mechanisms with multiplicative gating. It argues that gating expands attention from flat statistical manifolds to curved ones

topic_refs.headline: Gating Enables Curvature: A Geometric Expressivity Gap in Attention

items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Provides a geometric theory showing multiplicative gating expands attention from flat to curved statistical manifolds.

items.title: Expressivity of Transformers: A Tropical Geometry Perspective
items.summary_short: A research paper studies transformer expressivity using tropical geometry. It reports exact partition characterizations and scaling laws related to depth and attention heads

topic_refs.headline: Expressivity of Transformers: A Tropical Geometry Perspective

items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Derives transformer expressivity through tropical geometry, including exact partition characterizations and depth/head scaling laws.

items.title: Blazing the trails before beating the path: Sample-efficient Monte-Carlo planning
items.summary_short: A research paper presents a sample-efficient Monte Carlo planning method with guarantees tied to near-optimal reachable states. The result aims to reduce dependence on the full search-space size in planning analysis
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

topic_refs.headline: Blazing the trails before beating the path: Sample-efficient Monte-Carlo planning

items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Develops sample-efficient Monte Carlo planning with guarantees depending on near-optimal reachable states rather than full search space size.

items.title: Zeroth-Order Optimization at the Edge of Stability
items.summary_short: Zeroth-order (ZO) methods are widely used when gradients are unavailable or prohibitively expensive, including black-box learning and memory-efficient fine-tuning of large models, yet their optimization dynamics in deep learning remain underexplored.

items.title: Reinforcement Learning via Value Gradient Flow
items.summary_short: We study behavior-regularized reinforcement learning (RL), where regularization toward a reference distribution (the dataset in offline RL or the base model in LLM RL finetuning) is essential to prevent value over-optimization caused by erroneous out-of-distribution extrapolation.
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

items.title: Optimistic Policy Learning under Pessimistic Adversaries with Regret and Violation Guarantees
items.summary_short: Real-world decision-making systems operate in environments where state transitions depend not only on the agent's actions, but also on \textbf{exogenous factors outside its control}--competing agents, environmental disturbances, or strategic adversaries--formally, $s_{h+1} = f(s_h, a_h
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

items.title: Nautilus: An Auto-Scheduling Tensor Compiler for Efficient Tiled GPU Kernels
items.summary_short: We present Nautilus, a novel tensor compiler that moves toward fully automated math-to-kernel optimization. Nautilus compiles a high-level algebraic specification of tensor operators into efficient tiled GPU kernels.

items.title: Generalization in LLM Problem Solving: The Case of the Shortest Path
items.summary_short: Whether language models can systematically generalize remains actively debated. Yet empirical performance is jointly shaped by multiple factors such as training data, training paradigms, and inference-time strategies, making failures difficult to interpret.

items.title: ELMoE-3D: Leveraging Intrinsic Elasticity of MoE for Hybrid-Bonding-Enabled Self-Speculative Decoding in On-Premises Serving
items.summary_short: Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have become the dominant architecture for large-scale language models, yet on-premises serving remains fundamentally memory-bound as batching turns sparse per-token compute into dense memory activation.

items.title: World-Value-Action Model: Implicit Planning for Vision-Language-Action Systems
items.summary_short: Embodied world-model planning in VLA systems is a broadly active area, so this likely belongs in the daily hotspot feed too
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning
items.spotlight_comment: Embodied world-model planning in VLA systems is a broadly active area, so this likely belongs in the daily hotspot feed too.

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance Seed Debuts Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The company presented it as a major new foundation model release
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is signaling a broader foundation-model push across both general-purpose and speech-native systems, relevant for investors tracking Chinese frontier model competition and multimodal productization.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed released Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, giving an early public signal of the capabilities of its next-generation model line.
The company also introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, emphasizing attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural turn-taking in voice interaction.
The announcements arrived alongside ByteDance Seed’s 2027 foundation model campus recruitment push, indicating continued investment in long-horizon model research and talent acquisition.

evidence.title: ByteDance Seed 2027 Foundation Model Campus Recruitment Opens
Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena

featured_topics.headline: Claude Design Launches as Anthropic’s Visual Creation Product
featured_topics.summary_short: Anthropic launched Claude Design through Anthropic Labs. The product lets users collaborate with Claude to create visual outputs such as designs, prototypes, slides, and one-pagers
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Anthropic is expanding Claude into design and presentation workflows, a move that could increase enterprise seat value and put pressure on AI-native creative tooling vendors.
key_takeaways: Anthropic said Claude Design can generate polished visual outputs including designs, prototypes, slides, and one-pagers rather than only text responses.
The product is positioned for founders and product managers without formal design backgrounds, suggesting Anthropic is targeting business users beyond developers and knowledge workers.
Coverage from Anthropic and TechCrunch indicates this is a standalone Labs product, not just a feature update inside the core Claude assistant.

evidence.title: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs
Anthropic launches Claude Design, a new product for creating quick visuals

featured_topics.headline: Baidu Open-Sources ERNIE-Image, an 8B DiT Text-to-Image Model
featured_topics.summary_short: Baidu released ERNIE-Image, an open text-to-image generation model. The model is built on a single-stream Diffusion Transformer architecture with 8B DiT parameters
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If Baidu’s performance claims hold, ERNIE-Image strengthens the open-weight image generation stack and raises the competitive floor for proprietary multimodal model providers.
key_takeaways: Baidu described ERNIE-Image as an open text-to-image model built on a single-stream Diffusion Transformer architecture.
The company said the model uses 8B DiT parameters while reaching state-of-the-art performance among open-weight text-to-image models.
An 8B-parameter open release from a major Chinese lab could make high-end image generation more accessible for downstream fine-tuning, regional deployment, and commercial integration.

evidence.title: baidu/ERNIE-Image

topics.headline: Bringing AI-driven protein-design tools to biologists everywhere
topics.summary_short: Founded by Tristan Bepler PhD ’20 and former MIT professor Tim Lu PhD ’07, OpenProtein.AI offers researchers open-source models and other tools for protein engineering
